identity.name: Chiranjeet Banerjee
identity.title: Experience Design Lead / Manager
identity.summary: Experience Design Lead with 13+ years across UX, information architecture, research, and product design. Also creates editorial-style photography and AI-assisted visual art (synthography) under the name Broekn Camera.

blisseCode.name: Blisse Code
blisseCode.focus: UX strategy, AI integration consulting, design audits, and productized research services

broeknCamera.name: Broekn Camera
broeknCamera.focus: Premium cinematic portraiture and AI-assisted visual art with a neo-noir, emotionally raw aesthetic

approach: Evidence-first, then action: weighs options, notes risks, and decides deliberately
Mental models and IA before UI: models user thinking, structures content flows, then designs screens
Clear prioritization frameworks: RICE, Kano, MoSCoW, Opportunity Scoring
Revenue-aware design thinking: every design decision ties back to a measurable business question
Skeptical, evidence-led thinking: does not chase trends, focuses on what converts and retains

offerLadder.entry: Productized UX audit or mini strategy consult
offerLadder.mid: Workshops, consulting sprints, brand content packages
offerLadder.high: Premium commissioned shoots, retainers, enterprise consulting

values: Accuracy and skeptical thinking over motivational fluff
Premium and selective over mass volume
antiClaims: Does not overclaim results without evidence
Does not use fake testimonials or inflated credentials
Does not promise specific conversion lifts without data
Does not take on projects misaligned with quality standards
Does not do mass content farming or low-value work

faq.q: What kind of projects does Chiranjeet take on?
faq.a: Select consulting, audit, and creative projects. Focused on UX strategy, AI workflow integration, and premium visual work. Not available for low-budget or mass-market content work.

faq.q: Is Chiranjeet available for freelance work?
faq.a: Chiranjeet takes on select projects alongside full-time work. Best to book a 30-minute call to discuss scope and fit.

faq.q: What makes Chiranjeet different from other designers?
faq.a: The rare combination of UX leadership, AI workflow expertise, and cinematic visual storytelling. Evidence-led, revenue-aware thinking rather than trend-chasing.

faq.q: Does Chiranjeet work with international clients?
faq.a: Yes. Based in Kuala Lumpur but available for remote work across Southeast Asia and internationally.

faq.q: What tools and methods does Chiranjeet use?
faq.a: Figma for design, various research and synthesis methods including affinity mapping, card sorting, and usability testing. Uses RICE, Kano, and MoSCoW for prioritization. Design systems thinking with tokens and components.

faq.q: Can I check if Chiranjeet is a good fit for my role or project?
faq.a: Yes. Share the job description or project requirements and I can evaluate alignment with Chiranjeet's skills, experience, and interests. I will be honest about both fit and areas that may not align.

articles.title: The Compliance Flow and the Boardroom Are the Same Design Problem
articles.summary: Why the most important screen you will ever design is the one your stakeholder never sees. Explores how regulated product onboarding flows balloon from 4 steps to 15 screens through structural misalignment between compliance, product, and design.

articles.title: Thermodynamics of AI: Why You Are Exhausted
articles.summary: AI drove production cost to zero but verification cost stayed the same. Explains the Production-Verification Paradox and why senior professionals feel more depleted despite producing more artifacts than ever.

articles.title: The Cargo Cult of AI
articles.summary: Why prompt libraries are a trap and fluency is the only way out. Argues that treating AI with rigid templated scripts is the equivalent of cargo cult behavior.

notInPurview: Pure software engineering or backend development roles
Data science or ML engineering without design context
Junior or mid-level individual contributor positions
Marketing-only roles without product design overlap
Roles requiring relocation outside Southeast Asia (open to remote)
Mass-market content production or social media management
evaluationCriteria: Does the role involve design leadership, strategy, or systems thinking?
Is there cross-functional collaboration with product, engineering, and research?
Does it require evidence-based decision making rather than trend following?
Is there room for AI workflow integration or design innovation?
Does the scope match 13+ years of experience in UX, IA, and product design?
Is the work premium, selective, and quality-focused?

testimonials.name: Kathy C
testimonials.text: Chiranjeet embodies kindness and patience, creating a safe and comfortable environment where you can freely ask questions about UX and even providing mental support for managing stress and time effectively. He goes beyond being a UX mentor and extends his guidance to personal growth as well.

testimonials.name: Emilie C
testimonials.text: Chiranjeet would go above and beyond to help me become successful in UX design. With his feedback, I was able to grow my skill set quickly, and was able to achieve a job at Citi as a UX designer, during my schooling! He would get a 10 out of 5 star rating for me!

testimonials.name: Jessica Y
testimonials.text: Chiranjeet was very helpful throughout my early journey at UX Academy. He always provided advice to mitigate my worries as well as provided clarification on approaching certain steps in design whenever I needed it.

testimonials.name: Urisa P
testimonials.text: Being new to my design journey, I felt a lot of imposter syndrome, but Chiranjeet was easy to talk to and provided much objective, precise, and consistent feedback that helped me navigate this course. He is very knowledgeable in this field and provided invaluable insight into the industry.
